**New York's 15th congressional district** for the [United States House of Representatives](/wiki/United_States_House_of_Representatives "United States House of Representatives") is located in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), [State of New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"). The district has been represented by [Democrat](/wiki/United_States_Democratic_Party "United States Democratic Party") [Ritchie Torres](/wiki/Ritchie_Torres "Ritchie Torres") since 2021. At the time of his election, it was the poorest congressional district in the United States.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The 15th district is located entirely within [the Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx"), namely the southern portion of the [West Bronx](/wiki/West_Bronx "West Bronx") as well as the [South Bronx](/wiki/South_Bronx "South Bronx"). [Latinos](/wiki/Latinos "Latinos") make up the majority of the district's population, followed by Black people. Whites, Asians and other racial groups comprise a small minority. [Yankee Stadium](/wiki/Yankee_Stadium "Yankee Stadium") and the [Bronx Zoo](/wiki/Bronx_Zoo "Bronx Zoo") are both located within the district. The 15th district has the highest percentage of [Puerto Ricans](/wiki/Puerto_Ricans "Puerto Ricans") of any district in New York, and the second highest percentage of [Dominican Americans](/wiki/Dominican_Americans "Dominican Americans") of any district in New York, after the neighboring [13th congressional district](/wiki/New_York%27s_13th_congressional_district "New York's 13th congressional district").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York%27s_15th_congressional_district&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The district was a [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn")-based seat until 1982, when it was realigned to cover the East Side of [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"). Following the 1992 redistricting, it became the [upper Manhattan](/wiki/Upper_Manhattan "Upper Manhattan") seat previously designated the 19th District and the 18th District. After the 2012 redistricting, the 15th became [the Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx")'s primary district.

From 2003 to 2013 it was composed of [Upper Manhattan](/wiki/Upper_Manhattan "Upper Manhattan"), [Rikers Island](/wiki/Rikers_Island "Rikers Island") and a largely non-residential section of northwestern [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens") on the shore of the [East River](/wiki/East_River "East River") mostly occupied by a [Consolidated Edison](/wiki/Consolidated_Edison "Consolidated Edison") facility and a [New York Power Authority](/wiki/New_York_Power_Authority "New York Power Authority") [power plant](/wiki/Power_plant "Power plant"). The district included the neighborhoods of [Harlem](/wiki/Harlem "Harlem"), [Inwood](/wiki/Inwood,_Manhattan "Inwood, Manhattan"), [Marble Hill](/wiki/Marble_Hill,_Manhattan "Marble Hill, Manhattan"), [Spanish Harlem](/wiki/Spanish_Harlem,_Manhattan "Spanish Harlem, Manhattan"), [Washington Heights](/wiki/Washington_Heights,_Manhattan "Washington Heights, Manhattan"), [Morningside Heights](/wiki/Morningside_Heights,_Manhattan "Morningside Heights, Manhattan"), and portions of [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan") that included [Apollo Theater](/wiki/Apollo_Theater "Apollo Theater"), [Columbia University](/wiki/Columbia_University "Columbia University"), and [Grant's Tomb](/wiki/Grant%27s_Tomb "Grant's Tomb"). Much of that district is now the [13th district](/wiki/New_York%27s_13th_congressional_district "New York's 13th congressional district"), while the current 15th is essentially the successor of the former [16th district](/wiki/New_York%27s_16th_congressional_district "New York's 16th congressional district").
